target: data/wins.json WIN-053 detail_evidence (ii) sub-argument: add one-sentence foreclosure of magnitude-only re-interpretation using real-pole field inclination data

patches.description: ISS-2017: Add one sentence at the end of the (ii) sub-argument to foreclose the defender escape that B(r) is a magnitude-only expression (direction handled separately by toroidal flow). The rebuttal — from EXP defender_pivots_addressed #3 — is that measured field inclination at the geographic poles points oppositely, so even a magnitude-only reading still requires designating one aperture as sink. This was in the EXP but not the integrated paragraph.
patches.find_pattern: Re-interpreting the south coefficient relocates the contradictions; it does not eliminate them.
patches.replace_pattern: Re-interpreting the south coefficient relocates the contradictions; it does not eliminate them. The dome fits positive coefficient values to observed pole magnitudes; but measured field inclination at the geographic poles points oppositely — inward at one pole, outward at the other — so a magnitude-only re-interpretation still requires designating one aperture as the flux sink, which B(r) does not do.

tldr_evidence: ISS-2017: The (ii) sub-argument notes both apertures have positive-sign B(r) coefficients and thus the dome treats both as flux sources, which violates closed-circuit topology. A defender can claim B(r) is a magnitude expression, leaving direction to the toroidal-flow narrative. The EXP's defender_pivots_addressed #3 had the rebuttal (real-pole inclination is oppositely directed: inward at one, outward at the other), but this was not in the integrated paragraph. Adding it closes the magnitude-only escape without changing the argument structure.
tldr_verdict: Additive one-sentence patch. No verdict change; strengthens (ii) against a specific defender escape hatch by incorporating a rebuttal that was already in the EXP but not the rendered text.
